name: Open-label ambroxol showed CSF exposure and lysosomal target engagement in Parkinson disease
summary: Ambroxol gives the track a human lysosomal target-engagement foothold through GCase biology, but the study was open-label and disease-specific.
statement: In a 17-patient open-label Parkinson disease trial, high-dose oral ambroxol was reported as safe and well tolerated, reached cerebrospinal fluid, and achieved glucocerebrosidase-related target engagement.
population_or_model: Parkinson disease patients with and without GBA1 mutations
quantitative_note: Dose escalated to 1.26 g per day; 17 patients were included in the published open-label trial.
caveats: The trial was nonrandomized and noncontrolled.
The population was Parkinson disease, not a healthy-aging or geroscience cohort.
Biomarker engagement does not establish disease modification or functional aging benefit.
